2604.09514.title: Many Ways to Be Fake: Benchmarking Fake News Detection Under Strategy-Driven AI Generation
2604.09514.paper_summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have enabled the large-scale generation of highly fluent and deceptive news-like content. While prior work has often treated fake news detection as a binary classification problem, modern fake news increasingly arises through human-AI collaboration, where strategic inaccuracies are embedded within otherwise accurate and credible narratives. These mixed-truth cases represent a realistic and consequential threat, yet they remain underrepresented in existing benchmarks. To address this gap, we introduce MANYFAKE, a synthetic benchmark containing 6,798 fake news articles generated through multiple strategy-driven prompting pipelines that capture many ways fake news can be constructed and refined. Using this benchmark, we evaluate a range of state-of-the-art fake news detectors. Our results show that even advanced reasoning-enabled models approach saturation on fully fabricated stories, but remain brittle when falsehoods are subtle, optimized, and interwoven with accurate information.
2604.09514.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展使得能夠大規模生成高度流暢且具有欺騙性的新聞類內容。<lb/>雖然之前的研究通常將假新聞檢測視為一個二元分類問題，但現代假新聞越來越多地通過人類與人工智慧的合作產生，其中策略性的不準確性被嵌入在其他準確且可信的敘事中。<lb/>這些混合真相的案例代表了一種現實且具有重大影響的威脅，但在現有基準中仍然未得到充分代表。<lb/>為了解決這一空白，我們引入了MANYFAKE，一個包含6,798篇假新聞文章的合成基準，這些文章是通過多個策略驅動的提示管道生成的，捕捉了假新聞可以被構建和完善的多種方式。<lb/>利用這個基準，我們評估了一系列最先進的假新聞檢測器。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，即使是具有先進推理能力的模型在完全虛構的故事上接近飽和，但在虛假信息微妙、優化且與準確信息交織時仍然顯得脆弱。
2604.09514.author: Xinyu Wang et.al.
2604.09514.authors: Xinyu Wang, Sai Koneru, Wenbo Zhang, Wenliang Zheng, Saksham Ranjan, Sarah Rajtmajer
